title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame as a coach with Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett as presenters.
Rivers has 1,194 NBA coaching wins, ranking sixth all-time, and led the Celtics to the 2008 championship.
The Hall of Fame ceremony takes place on August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield, including Rivers and other notable inductees.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed feeling 'weird' playing against former teammate Jaylen Brown, now with the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks after playing with the Celtics for nine years.
The trade sparked strong reactions from Celtics fans, including viral emotional responses and social media tributes.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's Bill Barnwell suggests Patriots QB Drake Maye could be worth six first-round picks in a hypothetical trade.
Maye, 23, has achievements including a Super Bowl appearance and two Pro Bowl selections, making him highly valuable.
The article compares Maye's potential trade value to historical NFL quarterback trades involving Deshaun Watson and Eli Manning.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The Lake Winnipesaukee island home in Gilford, NH, features 1,037 square feet, three bedrooms, and one bath.
The cottage, built in 1952, offers lake views and short-term rental potential, with annual taxes of $8,027.
Listed for $889,500, the furnished home provides direct lake access and private sewer/water services.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' stars Matt Damon as Odysseus, focusing on human elements over mythology.
The film employs a non-linear narrative, typical of Nolan's style, exploring Odysseus' journey homeward.
Anne Hathaway plays Penelope and Tom Holland is Telemachus, grappling with suitors in Odysseus' absence.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle and his partner Devin married during the All-Star break.
The couple celebrated their wedding at Arya Trattoria, a restaurant in Boston's North End.
Tolle, a 23-year-old pitcher drafted in 2024, has an 18-start record with a 5-7 record and a 3.59 ERA.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore lead the Patriots' defensive line, combining for 86 pressures last season.
The Patriots allowed 101.7 rushing yards per game in 2025, ranking sixth-best in the NFL for run defense.
New England lost depth after Khyiris Tonga's departure, emphasizing the need for interior line depth behind Williams and Barmore.

title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam, 28, died in a skydiving incident in Orange, Massachusetts, due to a gust of wind.
The accident occurred at Jumptown Skydiving, which attributed it to an unusual weather event known as a dust devil.
Gaddam, a Northeastern alum and software developer, recently received his skydiving A License and lived in Boston.

title: Is the real summer turnaround in Boston sports coming from Legacy FC?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC achieved their first road win against Orlando Pride with a 1-0 score.
Legacy FC has made a midseason turnaround with recent wins, improving from their initial 0-5 start.
The team faces a critical match against second-place Washington Spirit on Sunday at Centreville Bank Stadium.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 16, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Nana's Green Tea opens July 17 in Back Bay, offering Japanese matcha, dessert drinks, and savory dishes.
C'Yool Yemeni Café and Bakery opens in July at South End, featuring coffee, flatbreads, and Yemeni pastries.
Zinneken’s Belgian Waffles expands to Boston Common with a new location opening winter 2027.
